title: Empty nullable value should not be accessed
description: Empty nullable value should not be accessedAnalyze your codeintentionality-completereliabilityBugcwesymbolic-executionWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoNullable value typescan hold either a value ornull.The value held in the nullable type can be accessed with theValueproperty or by casting it to the underlying type. Still, both operations throw anInvalidOperationExceptionwhen the value isnull. A nullable type should always be tested before accessing the value to avoid raising exceptions.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

noncompliant:
int? nullableInt = null;
int value = nullableInt.Value; // Noncompliant - accessing null nullable
compliant:
int? nullableInt = null;
int value = nullableInt.GetValueOrDefault(); // Compliant

int? nullableInt = null;
int value = nullableInt ?? 0; // Compliant

logic.detection_conditions: Methods where null nullable values are created and accessed without checking HasValue
logic.triggering_conditions: (nullable assigned null) AND (accessed via .Value or cast) AND (NOT guarded by HasValue)
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes accesses guarded by HasValue, GetValueOrDefault usage, null coalescing, nullables with values
logic.never_trigger_on: Accesses within HasValue checks, GetValueOrDefault() calls, null coalescing operators (??), nullables initialized with values
logic.reasoning: Accessing .Value on null nullable throws InvalidOperationException. Detects simple pattern: var x = (int?)null; ... x.Value. This is simplified check for same-method obvious cases. Excludes HasValue checks, GetValueOrDefault, and ?? operators.

should_trigger: int? x = null; int y = x.Value;
int? x = null; var y = x.Value.ToString();
should_not_trigger: int? x = null; if (x.HasValue) { int y = x.Value; }
int? x = null; int y = x.GetValueOrDefault();
int? x = null; int y = x ?? 0;

checks.message: Check HasValue before accessing nullable Value property
Check HasValue before casting nullable to value type
